task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris main menu or application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The puzzle category is accessed in GCompris
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'puzzle' section (typically indicated by a puzzle piece icon) is selected and displaying its activities.

task_completion.description: The 'Mirror the given image' activity is opened
task_completion.visual_evidence: The activity interface is visible, showing a grid with a pattern on one side of a dividing line and an empty or partially filled grid on the other side.

task_completion.description: The mirror image puzzle is completed correctly
task_completion.visual_evidence: The grid shows a perfectly symmetrical image on both sides of the mirror line, and/or a success indicator (like a smiling flower or transition to the next level) is visible.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the solved state is saved to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing a screenshot command (e.g., scrot, gnome-screenshot) targeting '/home/ga/mirror_solved.png', a GUI screenshot tool saving to this path, or a file manager displaying the 'mirror_solved.png' file in the '/home/ga/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GCompris application
integrity.visual_evidence: The GCompris interface is fully rendered as a native application, not an image opened in a viewer or a web browser.

integrity.description: The puzzle was solved by interacting with the grid
integrity.visual_evidence: Intermediate screenshots show partial progress of the grid being filled in, indicating manual GUI interaction rather than loading a pre-solved state.

integrity.description: The saved screenshot file contains the actual solved puzzle
integrity.visual_evidence: If the saved image is opened or previewed, it clearly shows the GCompris window with the solved mirror puzzle, not a generic or unrelated image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct activity in GCompris is internally named 'redraw_symmetrical' and titled 'Mirror the given image'. It is located in the 'puzzle' section. There is no activity explicitly named 'Reflections'. The expected output file must be saved exactly at /home/ga/mirror_solved.png.
